steps.title: Identify the direct causal link
steps.content: The passage explicitly states that Knight imitated Hair's fast painting technique, which “accounts in part for the impressionistic qualities that are now synonymous with the group's shared aesthetic.”

steps.title: Apply the causal relationship
steps.content: This directly implies choice A: Knight's paintings would embody those impressionistic qualities that characterize the Highwaymen group's aesthetic, making them more typical of the group than Reagan's detail-focused works. The other three either contradict what the passage states or introduce unsupported value judgments about quality. That matches choice A — A is correct.
